programme.title: Mysteries Unearthed With Danny Trejo
programme.desc: From a bat swung by one of baseball's boldest pioneers to an iconic painting hidden behind a false wall, these finds prove that America's greatest treasures can turn up in the most unlikely places.

programme.title: The Unbelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: From a man blasted by a high-pressure air hose to a lumberjack who survived near decapitation, these are mishaps that are so wild, they border on the impossible.

programme.title: The Mega-Brands That Built The World
programme.desc: In the late 1800s Americans rarely bathed, lived in filthy conditions and deadly diseases were commonplace. But James Gamble and Harley Procter invent a revolutionary new soap that revolutionises America.

programme.title: The Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Danielle geek out over a collection of oddball antiques in Ohio, while Robbie takes a thrilling joyride in his new vintage Impala, leaving him stranded.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: In the 21st century, UFOs have gone from fringe science to mainstream obsession. A new generation of UFO hunters is employing technology, and UFOs are being openly investigated by the government.

programme.title: Mysteries Unearthed With Danny Trejo
programme.desc: From a bat swung by one of baseball's boldest pioneers to an iconic painting hidden behind a false wall, these finds prove that America's greatest treasures can turn up in the most unlikely places.

programme.title: The Mega-Brands That Built The World
programme.desc: In the late 1800s Americans rarely bathed, lived in filthy conditions and deadly diseases were commonplace. But James Gamble and Harley Procter invent a revolutionary new soap that revolutionises America.

programme.title: History's Greatest Escapes With Morgan Freeman
programme.desc: During the Civil War, a daring prison break frees hundreds of Union soldiers. When he was captured and sent to Libby Prison in Richmond, Virginia, he engineered one of the greatest mass escapes in history.

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.sub-title: The Search For Alexander The Great
programme.desc: The most celebrated warrior king in history stirs fascination more than 2,000 years after his death. The location of his body is the subject of much debate. Where is the mummy of Alexander the Great.

programme.title: The Unbelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: Would you believe that the beginnings of modern endoscopy were really all thanks to one professional sword-swallower? Or that lack of sleep could have some strange upsides.

programme.title: The Unxplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: The world is full of stunning natural phenomena that are so strange they defy conventional wisdom. Can science explain Earth's mysteries? Or does human knowledge of the Earth only scratch the surface.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Things go nuclear when a Japanese Godzilla toy hits the counter. Can Corey come out on top before he's crushed? Chum does battle over a leather jacket from WWII, but will his offer bomb.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: There's no horsing around when a statue made of turquoise moseys on in. Will it be a yay, or a neigh for Rick? Then, Chum spins over to LA to have a record collection appraised.

programme.title: How Disney Built America
programme.desc: For nearly 70 years, Disney has pushed the boundaries of art and science through the work of a team of dreamers, designers, and builders known as the Imagineers.

programme.title: The Food That Built The World
programme.desc: Four intrepid food entrepreneurs race to harness emerging technologies like the home freezer and microwave, to bring revolutionary frozen snack icons to a waiting world, and forever change what we eat.

programme.title: The Food That Built Asia
programme.desc: Ironically, many components of Malaysia's national dish, Nasi Lemak are NOT even from Malaysia! Let's take a look at how this old rice dish came to be and how its evolution tells the history of the nation.

programme.title: The Food That Built Asia
programme.desc: The Food That Built Asia takes a look at the history behind nasi goreng, a Southeast Asian fried rice dish cooked with pieces of meat and vegetables.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Things go nuclear when a Japanese Godzilla toy hits the counter. Can Corey come out on top before he's crushed? Chum does battle over a leather jacket from WWII, but will his offer bomb.

programme.title: How Disney Built America
programme.desc: For nearly 70 years, Disney has pushed the boundaries of art and science through the work of a team of dreamers, designers, and builders known as the Imagineers.

programme.title: The Food That Built The World
programme.desc: Four intrepid food entrepreneurs race to harness emerging technologies like the home freezer and microwave, to bring revolutionary frozen snack icons to a waiting world, and forever change what we eat.

programme.title: The Unbelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: Would you believe that the beginnings of modern endoscopy were really all thanks to one professional sword-swallower? Or that lack of sleep could have some strange upsides.

programme.title: The Unxplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: The world is full of stunning natural phenomena that are so strange they defy conventional wisdom. Can science explain Earth's mysteries? Or does human knowledge of the Earth only scratch the surface.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: There's no horsing around when a statue made of turquoise moseys on in. Will it be a yay, or a neigh for Rick? Then, Chum spins over to LA to have a record collection appraised.

programme.title: The Food That Built Asia
programme.desc: Ironically, many components of Malaysia's national dish, Nasi Lemak are NOT even from Malaysia! Let's take a look at how this old rice dish came to be and how its evolution tells the history of the nation.

programme.title: The Food That Built Asia
programme.desc: The Food That Built Asia takes a look at the history behind nasi goreng, a Southeast Asian fried rice dish cooked with pieces of meat and vegetables.

programme.title: The Unbelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: Would you believe that the beginnings of modern endoscopy were really all thanks to one professional sword-swallower? Or that lack of sleep could have some strange upsides.

programme.title: The Unxplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: The world is full of stunning natural phenomena that are so strange they defy conventional wisdom. Can science explain Earth's mysteries? Or does human knowledge of the Earth only scratch the surface.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Things go nuclear when a Japanese Godzilla toy hits the counter. Can Corey come out on top before he's crushed? Chum does battle over a leather jacket from WWII, but will his offer bomb.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: There's no horsing around when a statue made of turquoise moseys on in. Will it be a yay, or a neigh for Rick? Then, Chum spins over to LA to have a record collection appraised.

programme.title: How Disney Built America
programme.desc: For nearly 70 years, Disney has pushed the boundaries of art and science through the work of a team of dreamers, designers, and builders known as the Imagineers.

programme.title: The Food That Built The World
programme.desc: Four intrepid food entrepreneurs race to harness emerging technologies like the home freezer and microwave, to bring revolutionary frozen snack icons to a waiting world, and forever change what we eat.

programme.title: The Food That Built Asia
programme.desc: Ironically, many components of Malaysia's national dish, Nasi Lemak are NOT even from Malaysia! Let's take a look at how this old rice dish came to be and how its evolution tells the history of the nation.

programme.title: The Food That Built Asia
programme.desc: The Food That Built Asia takes a look at the history behind nasi goreng, a Southeast Asian fried rice dish cooked with pieces of meat and vegetables.

programme.title: The Unbelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: Would you believe that the beginnings of modern endoscopy were really all thanks to one professional sword-swallower? Or that lack of sleep could have some strange upsides.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: There's no horsing around when a statue made of turquoise moseys on in. Will it be a yay, or a neigh for Rick? Then, Chum spins over to LA to have a record collection appraised.

programme.title: The Food That Built The World
programme.desc: Four intrepid food entrepreneurs race to harness emerging technologies like the home freezer and microwave, to bring revolutionary frozen snack icons to a waiting world, and forever change what we eat.

programme.title: The Unxplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: The world is full of stunning natural phenomena that are so strange they defy conventional wisdom. Can science explain Earth's mysteries? Or does human knowledge of the Earth only scratch the surface.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Things go nuclear when a Japanese Godzilla toy hits the counter. Can Corey come out on top before he's crushed? Chum does battle over a leather jacket from WWII, but will his offer bomb.

programme.title: History's Greatest Escapes With Morgan Freeman
programme.desc: A lifelong escape artist, Forrest Tucker, ends up in San Quentin prison. But despite his advanced age, he manages to engineer one of the most audacious prison breaks of all time.

programme.title: Mysteries Unearthed With Danny Trejo
programme.desc: From a bat swung by one of baseball's boldest pioneers to an iconic painting hidden behind a false wall, these finds prove that America's greatest treasures can turn up in the most unlikely places.

programme.title: Lost U-Boats Of Wwii
programme.desc: After finding Nazi artifacts at a sunken pier, Darrell continues his search to a site known as Target 3 where they find a possible component of a Nazi secret weapon that changes their investigation.

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: For centuries, this 500,000-square-mile area of the Atlantic has been the most notorious stretch of ocean on Earth. It's a place where ships, planes, and unsuspecting travellers have been lost.

programme.title: The Secret Of Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: Dr. Travis Taylor relives the most shocking and compelling phenomena that have taken place in the past year on Skinwalker Ranch.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Throughout ancient cultures, there are stories of god-like beings providing instruction to early humans, helping our ancestors to establish the first civilisations and teaching them. Could it be true.

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion
programme.desc: Bill and Tes battle The Everglades heat. Troy and Pickle search for a python paradise. A special day for Dusty goes awry while Zak and Bruce try their luck in The Dead Zone.

programme.title: Lost U-Boats Of Wwii
programme.desc: After finding Nazi artifacts at a sunken pier, Darrell continues his search to a site known as Target 3 where they find a possible component of a Nazi secret weapon that changes their investigation.

programme.title: The Secret Of Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: Dr. Travis Taylor relives the most shocking and compelling phenomena that have taken place in the past year on Skinwalker Ranch.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Throughout ancient cultures, there are stories of god-like beings providing instruction to early humans, helping our ancestors to establish the first civilisations and teaching them. Could it be true.

programme.desc: A lifelong escape artist, Forrest Tucker, ends up in San Quentin prison. But despite his advanced age, he manages to engineer one of the most audacious prison breaks of all time.
